architecture.summary: The paper studies unfaithfulness in Patchscopes, a framework that uses LLMs themselves to decode and explain hidden representations by patching internal activations into a target prompt. The authors identify that model bias from imbalanced linguistic patterns causes LLMs to override contextual information in patched representations during generation. They propose BALOR (Bias Alignment through Logit Recalibration), an inference-time decoding method that contrasts logits from an unpatched contrastive prompt against logits from a patched target prompt, recalibrating the logit distribution to suppress bias and amplify contextual information without modifying model parameters.

biblio.title: Faithful-Patchscopes: Understanding and Mitigating Model Bias in Hidden Representations Explanation of Large Language Models

claims.statement: Faithfulness of Patchscopes decreases by 11.84% to 28.47% under biased linguistic patterns, with an average 18.84% decrease
Imbalanced linguistic patterns strongly predict model bias: every standard deviation increase in frequency difference increases odds of bias by a factor of 9.338

claims.dataset: biased color, gender, culture, age tasks
claims.statement: BALOR achieves up to 33% relative performance improvement over vanilla Patchscopes
BALOR Show Rate (SR) in zero-shot color task: Llama2-7b 49.39%, Llama3.2-1b 71.66%, Llama3-8b 56.42%, Qwen3-4b 73.45% (BALOR Shared mode)
BALOR Show Rate (SR) in few-shot color task: Llama2-7b 52.10%, Llama3.2-1b 69.77%, Llama3-8b 47.62%, Qwen3-4b 68.90% (BALOR Shared mode)

Model bias arises from imbalanced linguistic patterns: logistic regression correlation (Spearman ρ) between logit difference and gradient similarity alignment shows weak correlation (p=0.001), indicating complementary metrics

claims.dataset: biased color task, all four models
claims.statement: BALOR Shared mode is stable across sampling temperatures (0.0 to 1.6) and hyperparameter α values, consistently outperforming vanilla Patchscopes; Divided mode exhibits higher variability

claims.dataset: zero-shot and few-shot biased color, gender, culture, age tasks
claims.statement: Prompt-based debiasing methods (Closed-Book, Internal-Evidence, Data-Bound) show unstable and inconsistent performance across tasks; fine-tuning-based methods (SFT) degrade substantially when biased training data conflicts with evaluation data
